finding: detection.rs moved BYTE-FOR-BYTE (0 edits) — `diff <(git show 4a2391732:server/src/modules/hardware/detection.rs) sdk/crates/ziee-hardware/src/detection.rs` empty. types.rs differs by ONLY `crate::sse_event_enum!`→`ziee_core::sse_event_enum!`; permissions.rs by ONLY the PermissionCheck import; monitoring.rs by ONLY `collect_hardware_usage` pub(super)→pub. All four semantically identical.

The sse_event_enum! macro is #[macro_export] with a $crate::macros::pascal_to_camel_case body, so invoking it as ziee_core::sse_event_enum! expands IDENTICALLY to the app's crate::sse_event_enum! (both resolve $crate=ziee_core). SSEHardwareUsageEvent's variants, event_name()/data() methods, and Into<axum::sse::Event> impl are unchanged.

schemars keys every moved type (OperatingSystemInfo/CPUInfo/MemoryInfo/GPU*/HardwareUsageUpdate/SSEHardwareUsageEvent/…) by short ident, unchanged by the crate move. Regenerated ui+desktop: types.ts BYTE-IDENTICAL, openapi.json canonically-equal (jq -S) vs baseline. Generated paths reverted via git checkout.

The gpu-detect feature + 4 optional GPU deps (nvml-wrapper/opencl3/ash/wgpu-hal) moved to ziee-hardware; server's own gpu-detect rewritten to ["ziee-hardware/gpu-detect"] and the 4 direct deps dropped. Grep proved those crates are used ONLY by detection.rs. ziee's default = [gpu-detect,…] forwards, so `cargo check -p ziee` (default features) compiles the exact #[cfg(feature="gpu-detect")] branches; desktop inherits ziee's default. dlopen GPU detect stays ON.

collect_hardware_usage widened pub(super)→pub — the MINIMUM to let ziee's retained handlers.rs:176 (`super::monitoring::collect_hardware_usage`) reach it across the crate boundary (E0603 without it). Pure visibility change; body/signature unchanged. Grep confirmed it was the ONLY pub(super)/pub(crate) item; detect_gpu_devices/add_client/remove_client/start_/stop_hardware_monitoring were already pub.

hardware/mod.rs `pub use ziee_hardware::{detection, monitoring, permissions, types};` aliases the four modules so every super:: path in the retained handlers/routes resolves, plus main.rs:469 `modules::hardware::monitoring::stop_hardware_monitoring()`. handlers/routes stay pub mod. ziee + ziee-desktop compile exit 0; zero call-site edits outside mod.rs+Cargo.toml.

detection.rs's trusted-binary resolution (resolve_system_binary over a fixed TRUSTED_DIRS allowlist, refusing PATH — 12-hardware F-06) moved byte-for-byte; monitoring.rs's MAX_SSE_CLIENTS=256 cap (F-01) + the AtomicBool compare_exchange single-spawn (F-04 TOCTOU) moved byte-for-byte. The admin-only tripwire warn in the retained handlers.rs (F-02) is unaffected (handler stayed).

ziee-hardware Cargo.toml deps (ziee-core/ziee-identity + serde/serde_json/schemars/axum/sysinfo/tokio/uuid/lazy_static/chrono/tracing + gpu-detect optionals + serial_test dev) version-match the ziee server catalog → single src-app/Cargo.lock unifies. Edition 2024 (detection.rs uses let-chains). sdk `cargo check --workspace` exit 0; ziee/ziee-desktop exit 0.

ziee-hardware names NO app type + NO DB: types=serde/schemars/axum(sse); detection=std::process/serde_json/GPU-libs; monitoring=axum/sysinfo/tokio/uuid/lazy_static; permissions=ziee_identity. No crate::modules, no sqlx/query!, no build.rs. The handlers/routes (RequirePermissions resolver + crate::common) + AppModule registration correctly stayed app-side. Genuinely reusable + build-DB-free.

cargo test -p ziee-hardware --features gpu-detect => 11 passed, 1 ignored (10 monitoring: add_client/remove cap+free ×3, broadcast delivers+prunes, collect_hardware_usage wellformed ×2, start idempotent/idle-stops; 2 detection under gpu-detect: detect_gpu_devices_returns_well_formed_rows, get_gpu_usage_data_percentages_in_range). Moved verbatim (only serial_test dev-dep added). No assertion edited.

No NEW warnings in the app build. With gpu-detect OFF (sdk --workspace default) an unused-mut warning appears at detection.rs:99 — a PRE-EXISTING conditional-compilation artifact (the mut binding is used only on the gpu-detect branch), identical to the app's own behavior when built --no-default-features. No build DB introduced (0-migration module).

cargo check -p ziee-desktop exit 0; desktop --generate-openapi yields types.desktop.ts BYTE-IDENTICAL + openapi.desktop.json canonically-equal vs baseline. Desktop inherits ziee's default features (gpu-detect on) via `ziee = { path }`, so the forwarded feature + detection paths are reached identically.
